items.title: Health Workers Need Clarity, Not ‘Strategic Directions’ — Clinician
items.summary: Health frontliners don't need more frameworks. We don't need more “strategic directions” or “ongoing engagements.” Many of us don't like fluff; we don't need unnecessary content packaged as reform. We need clarity, alignment, timelines and decisions. The post Health Workers Need Clarity, Not ‘Strategic Directions’ — Clinician appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Anwar: Only ‘Luxurious’ Scope Of Hospital Projects Targeted In Spending Cuts
items.summary: On health spending cuts, PM Anwar Ibrahim says only the "more luxurious" scope of hospital projects will be "reduced slightly". It's unclear if development expenditure is also targeted in the government's austerity drive, besides operational expenditure. The post Anwar: Only ‘Luxurious’ Scope Of Hospital Projects Targeted In Spending Cuts appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Health Service Commission May Not Necessarily Solve HR Problems — Dr KA
items.summary: A Health Service Commission (SPK) isn't necessarily the solution to HR problems in the health service because if it follows the Education Service Commission's model, the SPK won't have the power to create new positions or autonomy of in-service training. The post Health Service Commission May Not Necessarily Solve HR Problems — Dr KA appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Health Workers Brace For Death By A Thousand Cuts
items.summary: Pharmacists expect a reduction in drug dispensing supply and cuts to more effective medicines, ahead of plans to gut health spending by RM4.65 billion (10% of MOH’s budget). Doctors refuse to work for free if on-call claims are barred in allowance cuts. The post Health Workers Brace For Death By A Thousand Cuts appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Cut Waste If We Must, But Don’t Cut Protection
items.summary: The wrong health spending cuts will cost Malaysia more than they save. Don't touch primary care, emergency services, public health/communicable disease control, essential medicines, and MCH. Older Malaysians will be among the first to suffer consequences. The post Cut Waste If We Must, But Don’t Cut Protection appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Dialysis, Cancer Patient Groups Oppose Health Spending Cuts
items.summary: NKF and NCSM warn that cuts to the government's health budget may hit dialysis and cancer services. NKF, which receives substantial subsidies from MOH for kidney replacement therapy, says it may be forced to reduce screening and prevention programmes. The post Dialysis, Cancer Patient Groups Oppose Health Spending Cuts appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Dzulkefly Expects 10% Cut To Health Ministry’s Budget
items.summary: Dr Dzul says he expects a cut of about 10% to MOH's RM46.5 billion budget, but maintains that "essential core services" will be protected. A 10% cut is equivalent to RM4.65 billion, much higher than the RM3 billion cut initially suggested by the Treasury. The post Dzulkefly Expects 10% Cut To Health Ministry’s Budget appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Pharmacies Barred From Blood Taking, Says Health Ministry
items.summary: MOH says blood taking is not permitted in community pharmacies because phlebotomy is a clinical procedure that can only be carried out in licensed health care facilities under Act 586, adding that it will continue to strengthen enforcement. The post Pharmacies Barred From Blood Taking, Says Health Ministry appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Finance Ministry Says Only Targeting ‘Non-Critical’ Spending Cuts
items.summary: MOF says it's only targeting "non-critical" spending cuts and core health expenditure will still continue. But MOF didn't revoke its RM3 billion spending cut request to MOH. Instead, MOF cites postponing events, limiting overseas trips and utility savings. The post Finance Ministry Says Only Targeting ‘Non-Critical’ Spending Cuts appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Health Spending Isn’t A Cost, But Core Public Investment — Dr Kelvin Yii
items.summary: Health spending cuts may worsen the health workforce crisis, especially in Sabah and Sarawak. MOH must never be seen as an easy target for savings. Health spending isn’t a cost to minimise, but a core public investment. MOF must reconsider the cuts. The post Health Spending Isn’t A Cost, But Core Public Investment — Dr Kelvin Yii appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Malaysian Haj pilgrims urged to heed health advice amid rising heat in Saudi Arabia, says minister
items.summary: NILAI, May 4 — Malaysian Haj pilgrims have been advised to follow the advice of doctors, health officers and Lemba...

items.title: Anwar says austerity measures won’t hit healthcare or hiring of new doctors
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 4 — Austerity measures that could see billions of ringgit slashed from several ministries will not...
